events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario is about a panelboard installation where neutrals and grounds are mixed in a subpanel. This is a common code violation.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 60,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Neutrals and equipment grounds are mixed on the same bar in the subpanel, creating an objectionable current path and bond violation",
    "Subpanel lacks separate neutral bar and equipment grounding bar as required for a panel fed from a separately derived or four-wire feeder",
    "Neutral-ground bond in a subpanel can energize equipment grounding conductors and raceway during fault or unbalanced load conditions"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize and lockout/tagout the panel before any correction",
    "Install and isolate a dedicated neutral bar, bonded to the enclosure only at the main service or separately derived source, not in the subpanel",
    "Install a separate equipment grounding bar bonded to the subpanel enclosure",
    "Terminate all neutrals on the isolated neutral bar and all equipment grounds on the grounding bar",
    "Verify working clearance per NEC 110.26 before re-energizing",
    "Inspect and torque all terminations after correction"
  ],
  "rationale": "Mixing neutrals and grounds in a subpanel violates NEC requirements for separate neutral and grounding conductors and creates objectionable current paths on grounding conductors, posing shock and fire hazards. The work is non-compliant and defective and must be corrected before energization.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Art. 408",
    "NEC 110.26",
    "NEC 200.4"
  ]
}
